title: Oberna macrocarpa
taxonomy: Oberna macrocarpa is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It is native to southern Africa and belongs to the genus Oberna. The plant is further classified under the kingdom Plantae, division Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Oberna macrocarpa is also known as a member of the family Pediculariaceae, however some sources also list it as a member of the family Scrophulariaceae. The classification of Oberna macrocarpa is subject to ongoing taxonomic revisions.

intro: Oberna macrocarpa, also known as the large-fruited Oberna, is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. The health benefits of Oberna macrocarpa include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Oberna macrocarpa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
